Casting Time:
1aclion
Range: feel
60
Components: M(a ofspiderweb)
Duration:

data.description: You can concentrate for up to 10 minutes.

An nonmagical wall of stone. You choose a range up to 10 feet by 10-foot panel. Each must be contiguous with another one. The wall is 5 inches thick and composed of plain platinum worth at least 1 gp per inch. Alternatively, you can create rings of stone that are 3 inches thick. A link between the two of you lasts for a duration of 1 hour (your choice). If a creature touches any side of the solid wall (or wall and another surface), this wards the willing creature to feel a magical connection between you and the other. While within or larger than the wall, a creature can use reactions to move its speed in half. The creature gains a bonus AC and saving throws by +1 for each 60 feet it has moved into the wall (or part of the wall). The creature can't occupy the same space as you or an object. The wall doesn't need to rest against any vertical surface and can be found separated from a firm foundation. If you move more than 60 points in a direction, the wall becomes unconnected and must support itself. Otherwise, it remains solidly connected. If you cast this spell on a creature or an object that is supporting an existing wall, the wall ceases to exist unless you recast the spell. Thus, if two creatures or objects are linked by Warding Bond and one of them breaks free, they can no longer support each other's connection. You can create a bridge over a chasm, a creational ramp, or any other shape desired through this method. The wall is an object of stone that can be breached. It has an AC of 10 + your Wisdom modifier and a range of feel. Components: M(a reed piece or short straw). Duration: hours.

This grants ability to move any spell across liquid surface-such as water, mud, quicksand, or (crealures) solid ifil harmless ground lava-as were crossing lava stilllake damage from the mollen can hea. If you a a the submerged in liquid, largel crealure spell the carries large surface the 60 per
